title: P. falciparum microhaplotype deep sequencing assays
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3108h","text":"This SOP is adapted from a previously established Plasmodium vivax high-throughput rhAmpSeq library preparation protocol developed by Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT)1. Building on this framework, a selection of markers from the two most widely used P. falciparum (Pf) genotyping panels, SpotMalaria2 (widely used in the Asia-Pacific; 137 markers) and MAD4HatTeR3 (widely used in Africa; 276 markers), were combined into a single P. falciparum panel comprising 237 markers across two multiplexes (A and B). The SOP describes the laboratory procedures for high-throughput amplification of the two multiplexes of 120 (A) and 117 (B) P. falciparum markers for downstream genotyping by sequencing on the Illumina platform. The 237 markers include a panel of 203 microhaplotype (loci comprising 3 or more globally high-diversity SNPs) distributed across the P. falciparum genome, 19 drug resistance markers, 10 diagnostic markers, and five Plasmodium species confirmation markers. Diagnostic markers are still in development, and caution should be taken when using them. It is advised to include positive, human and non-template controls in each run.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":50,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":251,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":431,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":632,"length":14},{"style":"italic","offset":854,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":936,"length":10},{"style":"sup","offset":167,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":300,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":363,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"flp5r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3d6i","text":"We welcome any questions and/or feedback on the protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Input DNA does not require prior clean up or quantification or normalization. If DNA amount is limited (11 μL of DNA is required for this protocol), gDNA can be diluted using IDTE, pH 8.0.

Thaw an aliquot of the following reagents from -20°C storage. o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – Forward Stock Pool (A) o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – Reverse Stock Pool (A) o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – Forward Stock Pool (B) o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – Reverse Stock Pool (B)
After thawing the primers, briefly vortex, then centrifuge.
Prepare working primer pools using a 1 in 5 dilution.

To sequence 96 samples: o 42μL of A Forward Stock Pool + 168μL of IDTE, pH 7.5 o 42μL of A Reverse Stock Pool + 168μL of IDTE, pH 7.5 o 42μL of B Forward Stoc k Pool + 168μL of IDTE, pH 7.5 o 42μL of B Reverse Stock Pool+ 168μL of IDTE, pH 7.5 Tip: Prepare multiple aliquots of rhAmpSeq working forward/reverse primer pools and store at -20°C to avoid multiple freeze-thaw cycles.

Thaw the following reagents completely to room temperature(15-20°C). o 4X rhAmpSeq Library Mix 1 o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – A Forward Working Pool o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – A Reverse Working Pool o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – B Forward Working Pool o Rebalanced rhAmpSeq PCR Panel – B Reverse Working Pool Important! It is important to perform this step in the morning, as there is no safe stopping point until the end of Step 47
After thawing the reagents, briefly vortex and then centrifuge.

Prepare the PCR_1 master mixes in a Lo-bind Eppendorf tube, separately for the A and B panels, corresponding to the number of samples being processed: Component Per reaction X 100 (for 96 samples) 1 4X rhAmpSeq Library Mix 1 5μL 500μL 2 10X rhAmpSeq pf custom PCR Panel – Forward Working Pool 2μL 200μL 3 10X rhAmpSeq pf custom PCR Panel – Reverse Working Pool 2μL 200μL 4 Total 9μL 900μL Targeted rhAmpSeq PCR_1 mastermix
Briefly vortex and centrifuge down the mastermix
Prepare two 96-well plates for the A and B panels. Add 9μL of PCR_1 master mix to the designated reaction well for each panel, ensuring the correct master mix is added to the correct plate.
Add 11μL of gDNA to the corresponding wells in both A and B panel plates.
Seal both plates, then briefly vortex and centrifuge.

Place the plates in thermocyclers and run the following program (Lid set to 105°C): Step Cycle Temperature Duration 1 Activate enzyme 1 95°C 10 min 2 Amplify 14 95°C 15 sec 3 61°C 8 min 4 Deactivate enzyme 1 99.5°C 15 min 5 Hold 1 4°C Hold Targeted rhAmpSeq PCR_1 program
Once the program is complete, remove the plate from the thermocycler and proceedimmediately to Dilute PCR_1 product .
Briefly vortex the PCR_1 product, then centrifuge.
In two new 96-well plates, add 95 μL of Nuclease-Free Water to each well, and label the plates as 1:20 dilution PCR_1 product panel A and panel B.
Transfer 5 μL of the PCR_1 product to each designated well of A and B panel plates (To prepare a final dilution of 1 in 20). Pipette up and down to ensure the entire volume is dispensed.
Seal the plates and thoroughly vortex to mix, then centrifuge before proceeding immediately to Perform Indexing PCR_2 .

Thaw 4X rhAmpSeq Library Mix 2 and xGen 10nt UDI Index Primer Working Plates completely to room temperature (15-20°C). Important! Stock xGen 10nt UDI Index Primer plates from IDT have Forward and Reverse primers combined at 20 μM total (10 μM each primer). To prepare a working plate (2 μM total), prepare a 1 in 10 dilution with IDTE, pH 7.5 (i.e., 2 μL of Stock Primer + 18 μL of IDTE, pH 7.5).
Briefly vortex, then centrifuge the reagents.

Prepare the PCR_2 master mix in a Lo-bind Eppendorf tube corresponding to the number of samples being processed: Component Per reaction X 196 ( for 96 samples in panel A and B) 1 4X rhAmpSeq Library Mix 2 5μL 980μL 2 Nuclease Free Water 2μL 392μL 3 Total 7μL 1372μL Targeted rhAmpSeq PCR_2 mastermix
In two new 96-well plates, add 7 μL of PCR_2 mastermix to each well.

Add 2 μL of xGen 10nt UDI Index Primer Working Concentration to each correspondingwell using a multichannel pipette Important! Note down the index plate, well position used for each sample and the PCR_1 pf panel name (A or B).
Add 11 μL of diluted rhAmpSeq PCR_1 product to the corresponding well using a multichannel pipette.
Seal the plates, then briefly vortex and centrifuge.

Place the plates in thermocyclers and run the following program (lid set to 105°C): Step Cycle Temperature Duration 1 Activate enzyme 1 95°C 3 min 2 Amplify 24 95°C 15 sec 3 60°C 30 sec 4 72°C 30 sec 5 Final extension 1 72°C 1 min 6 Hold 1 4°C Hold Targeted rhAmpSeq PCR_2 program
Once the program is complete, remove the plates from the thermocyclers and proceed immediately to Pool libraries .
Optional: PCR success can be confirmed by gel electrophoresis on 2% agarose gel after reaching the nearest safe stopping point.

Using a multichannel pipette, pool each row from designated A and B panel plates by transferring 5 μL of each indexed library into one 8-well strip tube (use 10 μL if the total number of individual libraries processed is less than 20 samples). Tip: Minimize sample-to-sample variability with precise pipetting.
Cap the strip tube, briefly vortex, then centrifuge down.
Combine all libraries from the strip tube into a single 1.5 or 2 mL LoBind tube. Important! Pool the complete volume from each well for an even sample coverage.
Properly seal and store the plates with remaining individual indexed libraries at -20°C then proceed immediately to clean up the pooled libraries.
Transfer 100 μLof the rhAmpSeq library pool to a new 1.5 mL tube.
Vortex the SPRI beads thoroughly before use.
Add 70 μL of SPRI (0.7X) to the library pool.
Thoroughly mix the contents of the tube by pipetting up and down.
Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Prepare 2 mL of fresh 80% ethanol solution by combining 400 μL of molecular-grade water and 1,600 μL of molecular-grade ethanol (Absolute ethanol).
Briefly pulse spin down the tube then place it on a magnetic rack for 5 minutes, or until the solution is clear.
Keeping the tube on the magnet, do the following:
Aspirate with the pipette, then discard the supernatant.
Add 1000 μL of 80% ethanol to the tube.
Incubate at room temperature for 30 seconds.
Aspirate, then discard the supernatant.
Repeat the 80% ethanol wash one more time ( steps 40.2 - 40.4 ).
Using a new P200 pipette tip, remove the remaining ethanol from the tube.
Allow the beads to dry for 3 minutes at room temperature.
Remove the tube from the magnet.
Add 22 μL of IDTE, pH 8.0, to elute the library pool.
Thoroughly vortex to fully resuspend the beads, then briefly pulse spin down the tube.
Incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes.
Place the tube on the magnet to collect the beads for 1 minute, or until the solution is clear.
While keeping the tube on the magnet, transfer 20 μL of the final library pool elution into a new 1.5 mL LoBind tube, ensuring no beads are carried over.
Proceed to quantify and check the size of the library.
Safe stopping point . Store any remaining library pool at -20°C for up to 3 weeks. using the stored library make sure to repeat the library QC steps.
Quantify the library concentration using Illumina NGS library quantification kit (such as The Invitrogen Collibri Library Quantification Kit).
Using capillary electrophoresis, check the fragment size of the library before and after beads clean-up to ensure that adapter dimers have been successfully removed.
Follow Illumina's recommended protocol for a 2 x 150bp paired-end sequencing run on your Illumina platform.

Pool the primers according to the calculations in the attached Excel file for each panel (Panel A and B) and each direction (forward and reverse) separately to prepare rebalanced primer Stock pools. Pf primer pool_calculation_final.xlsx
creator: Anjana Rai
